sections.sectionExcerpt: Found in large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats in South and South-east Asia. Known as Bhimanama in Kerala. Conservation status: WPA Schedule I, CITES Appendix II, IUCN Critically Endangered (CR). They are ambush predators with flattened heads.
evidenceBullets: IUCN Status is Critically Endangered (CR)
Listed in WPA, 1972 Schedule I and CITES Appendix II
Found in large lowland rivers, estuaries, and coastal mudflats in South and South-east Asia
Locally called Bhimanama in Kerala
Ambush predators with aggressive behavior and a lightning speed strike

sections.subtopic: Softshell Turtles and New Fish Species
sections.title: Labeo uru and Labeo chekida
sections.focus: Newly discovered freshwater fish species endemic to the Western Ghats river systems.
sections.rationale: New species discoveries in biodiversity hotspots (Western Ghats) are standard UPSC prelims targets, especially matching species with their river habitats.
sections.sectionExcerpt: ICAR-NBFGR discovered two new freshwater fish species endemic to Western Ghats river systems. Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River (originating from Pattimala hills, Karnataka). Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river (originating in Anamalai Hills).
evidenceBullets: Discovered by ICAR-National Bureau of Fish Genetic Resources (NBFGR)
Labeo uru is found in the Chandragiri River, a west-flowing interstate river
Labeo chekida inhabits the Chalakkudy river formed in the Anamalai Hills
Both are freshwater fish endemic to the Western Ghats river systems

sections.title: Clinidium lalitae
sections.focus: Discovery, characteristics, and ecological role of the newly identified beetle species.
sections.rationale: A specific, localized species discovery with distinct facts such as its location (Talle Valley WLS) and ecological role, suitable for a factual or matching question.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Scientists from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) identified a new species of beetle named Clinidium lalitae, in Talle Valley Wildlife Sanctuary, Arunachal Pradesh. It belongs to the subfamily Rhysodinae, a rare group of ground beetles inhibiting forest floors, and plays a significant ecological role in nutrient cycling and soil health.
evidenceBullets: Identified by ZSI in Talle Valley Wildlife Sanctuary, Arunachal Pradesh.
Belongs to subfamily Rhysodinae, a rare group of ground beetles.
Plays a significant ecological role in nutrient cycling and soil health.

sections.title: Lichens
sections.focus: Characteristics, distribution, ecological significance, and recent studies on Lichens.
sections.rationale: Highly examinable for UPSC due to core ecological concepts like symbiosis, bioindicators, and ecological succession, along with specific facts about India's first Lichen Park.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Lichens can survive and remain metabolically active under simulated Mars-like conditions. They cover 8% of land surface, including extreme environments. India hosts over 2,700 species, with its first Lichen Park established in 2020 in Munsiyari, Uttarakhand. Lichens are a symbiotic association between a fungus (mycobiont) and an alga or cyanobacterium (photobiont). They act as bioindicators of air pollution (especially SO2) and are pioneer species in ecological succession, aiding in soil formation.
evidenceBullets: Symbiotic association between fungus (mycobiont) and an alga or cyanobacterium (photobiont).
Bioindicators of air pollution, especially SO2 and heavy metals.
Pioneer species that act as first colonizers in ecological succession and aid in soil formation.
India's first Lichen Park was established in 2020 in Munsiyari, Uttarakhand.
Can survive and remain metabolically active under simulated Mars-like conditions.

sections.title: Tardigrades (Water Bear/Moss Piglet)
sections.focus: Characteristics, survival mechanisms (cryptobiosis), and space experiments involving Tardigrades.
sections.rationale: The unique survival mechanism of cryptobiosis and relevance to space missions make Tardigrades a prime candidate for science and environment questions.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla will take Tardigrades to the International Space Station (ISS) for an experiment. Also known as Water Bears or Moss Piglets, these half-millimetre long, eight-legged segmented micro-animals endure extreme temperatures, crushing pressures, and radiation through cryptobiosis. Cryptobiosis is a state of extreme inactivity in response to adverse environmental conditions. Recognized classes include Eutardigrada and Heterotardigrada.
evidenceBullets: Eight-legged segmented micro-animals, also known as Water Bears or Moss Piglets.
Endure extreme hot/cold temperatures, crushing pressures, and radiation through cryptobiosis.
Cryptobiosis is a state of extreme inactivity in response to adverse environmental conditions.
Being taken to the International Space Station (ISS) for experiments by Indian astronaut Shubhanshu Shukla.

sections.title: Cicadas
sections.focus: Habitat, life cycle, and unique characteristics of Cicadas.
sections.rationale: A small concept cluster focusing on their return to Silent Valley NP and their unique sound-producing mechanism, suitable for a single statement-based question.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Cicadas have returned to Kerala's Silent Valley National Park. They occur in warm and temperate biomes and have a life cycle of three stages: egg, nymph, and adult. Adult females usually lay eggs in woody plant tissues. Most species produce an exceptionally loud song by the rapid buckling and unbuckling of drum-like tymbals.
evidenceBullets: Returned to Kerala's Silent Valley National Park.
Life cycle consists of three stages: egg, nymph, and adult.
Produce exceptionally loud songs by the rapid buckling and unbuckling of drum-like tymbals.
Adult females usually lay eggs in woody plant tissues.

sections.title: Red Sanders (Red Sandalwood)
sections.focus: Habitat, characteristics, uses, and conservation status of Red Sanders.
sections.rationale: Highly examinable due to its IUCN Endangered status, CITES Appendix II listing, and specific endemic habitat in the Eastern Ghats.
sections.sectionExcerpt: National Biodiversity Authority sanctioned fund for Red Sanders Conservation under Access and Benefit Sharing (ABS) Mechanism. Native to Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh. Grows in dry deciduous forests between 150-900 meters altitude. Presence of red dye 'Santalin' imparts its characteristic red colour. Conservation Status: CITES Appendix II; IUCN Status: EN (Endangered).
evidenceBullets: Native to the Southern Eastern Ghats of Andhra Pradesh.
Grows in dry deciduous forests.
Contains the red dye 'Santalin'.
IUCN Status is Endangered (EN) and listed in CITES Appendix II.

sections.title: Banana Musa Indandamanensis
sections.focus: Discovery, physical characteristics, and genetic significance of the wild banana species Musa indandamanensis.
sections.rationale: Notable for its unique physical characteristics (longest infructescence, 11m height) and specific geographic origin in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
sections.sectionExcerpt: An infructescence of about 4.2 metres recorded in species of wild banana Musa indandamanensis, making it the longest infructescence recorded in bananas. First recorded from a remote tropical forest near Krishna Nala reserve forest in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in 2012. Unique green flowers; fruit bunch lux (axis) thrice size of regular banana species. 11 metres high. Natural genetic resource for plant breeders to develop high yielding and disease resistant varieties.
evidenceBullets: Discovered in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands in 2012.
Has the longest recorded infructescence in bananas at about 4.2 metres.
Grows up to 11 metres high and features unique green flowers.
Acts as a natural genetic resource for developing disease-resistant plant varieties.

sections.title: White Himalayan Lily
sections.focus: Characteristics, medicinal uses, and conservation status of the White Himalayan Lily.
sections.rationale: Important for UPSC due to its Critically Endangered (CR) IUCN status and specific medicinal properties.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Uttarakhand Forest Department’s latest report highlights conservation of White Himalayan Lily (Lilium polyphyllum). Rare and fragrant lily species native to Himalayan region. Known for white or pink flowers with dark spots and stripes. Rich in active chemical constituents like alkaloids and furocoumarins. Used for treating haematemesis, bronchitis, rheumatism, etc. IUCN Status: Critically Endangered (CR).
evidenceBullets: Native to the Himalayan region.
Rich in active chemical constituents like alkaloids and furocoumarins.
Used medicinally for treating haematemesis, bronchitis, and rheumatism.
IUCN Status is Critically Endangered (CR).

sections.title: Murikooti Pacha
sections.focus: Distribution, traditional names, and medicinal properties of Murikooti Pacha.
sections.rationale: A specific medicinal plant with a unique active compound (acteoside) and traditional significance (Vranaropani) that can be tested in science and technology or environment sections.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Indian researchers harnessed its wound-healing potential. Native to Southeast Asia, also found in other tropical regions including India. Red ivy plant, also known as Vranaropani in Sanskrit. Wound-healing pad containing, acteoside, a natural compound known for therapeutic potential.
evidenceBullets: Native to Southeast Asia and found in tropical regions including India.
Known as Vranaropani in Sanskrit.
Contains acteoside, a natural compound with significant wound-healing and therapeutic potential.

sections.title: Arogyapacha
sections.focus: Medicinal properties, Kani tribes, and the Jeevani drug.
sections.rationale: Distinct indigenous medicinal plant with specific tribal association (Kani) and a benefit-sharing model (Jeevani drug).
sections.sectionExcerpt: A rare, indigenous medicinal plant of India. Medicinal Properties: Anti-oxidant, antistress, anti-microbial, anti-inflammatory, anti-tumor, anti-ulcer, anti-diabetic etc. Famous for traditional use by Kani tribes, in Agastya hills, Western Ghats. 'Jeevani' Drug has been developed using 'Arogya Pacha'. Kani Tribes gets a share of 50% on commercial returns.
evidenceBullets: Indigenous medicinal plant of India.
Used traditionally by Kani tribes in Agastya hills, Western Ghats.
Used to develop the 'Jeevani' drug.
Kani tribes receive a 50% share of commercial returns.

sections.title: Conocarpus Trees
sections.focus: Invasive nature, environmental impact, and government bans.
sections.rationale: An invasive ornamental plant with significant negative ecological impacts (groundwater consumption, allergenic pollen) leading to state bans.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Recently government-appointed panel recommended Supreme Court to ban Conocarpus. (Banned earlier by Gujarat and Tamil Nadu). Has 2 species: One native to coastal areas of tropical America, and parts of West Africa. Other found in arid coastal zones of Somalia and Yemen... Ornamental plant provides green canopy... Invasive (also known as green desert); excessive groundwater consumption; as allergenic pollen, etc.
evidenceBullets: Banned by Gujarat and Tamil Nadu, with a panel recommending a Supreme Court ban.
Native to coastal areas of tropical America, Africa, and the Arabian Peninsula.
Invasive species known as 'green desert'.
Causes excessive groundwater consumption and produces allergenic pollen.

sections.title: Sea Buckthorn
sections.focus: Ecological significance, nutritional value, and space experiments.
sections.rationale: A high-altitude plant with unique ecological (pioneer species) and nutritional benefits, recently featured in ISS experiments.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Its seeds grown in Ladakh are part of experiments on board the International Space Station flown by NASA’s Crew-11 mission. Deciduous shrub/tree; Well-adapted to live in cold areas of high altitude. Significance: Pioneer species for soil improvement, wind & sand control, and soil & water conservation; High nutritional value (vitamins; carotenoids; polyphenols, etc.); Medicinal (antioxidant; anticancer, etc.)
evidenceBullets: Seeds grown in Ladakh used in experiments on the International Space Station (NASA Crew-11).
Deciduous shrub/tree adapted to cold, high-altitude areas.
Acts as a pioneer species for soil improvement, wind/sand control, and soil/water conservation.
High nutritional and medicinal value (antioxidant, anticancer).

sections.title: Senna Spectabilis
sections.focus: Invasive characteristics and eradication efforts in Kerala.
sections.rationale: A highly invasive species in the Western Ghats (Wayanad) that alters soil chemistry and chokes native flora.
sections.sectionExcerpt: Kerala carried out India’s first science-based, community-driven eradication of Senna Spectabilis in Wayanad. Invasive species, Native to American tropicals. Forms dense, sterile thickets, choking out native plants, altering soil chemistry and depriving herbivores of food, grows 7-18m tall. Resembles Kerala’s state flower kanikkonna.
evidenceBullets: India's first science-based, community-driven eradication carried out in Wayanad, Kerala.
Invasive species native to American tropicals.
Forms dense thickets that choke native plants and alter soil chemistry.
Resembles kanikkonna, the state flower of Kerala.
